bullets.text: Tehran has launched more strikes after explosions were reported in southern Iran, with Centcom confirming it hit 90 Iranian targets in the latest round of strikes. The Iranian health ministry reported 14 people were killed since Tuesday.

An LGBTQ+ cruise ship, the Scarlet Lady, was refused entry to Egypt, days after being turned away from Turkey. The 2,000 passengers were informed of the change, with one onboard expressing intentions to "sparkle and spend elsewhere."

Ukraine has reportedly struck Russian ships near Crimea, escalating attacks on fuel supplies in what appears to be the latest phase of efforts to disrupt routes and supplies into and out of occupied Crimea.
A major contractor on the Snowy Hydro project is defending itself against accusations of exposing five workers to the risk of death or serious injury in two separate incidents.
More than half of Australians are reportedly minimising heating in their homes to avoid high power bills during winter.
Telstra's national phone network experienced an outage caused by a 'digital domino chain' triggered by technology similar to that used for daylight savings adjustments.
The world is facing an increasing diesel supply crunch as Ukraine drone strikes have reportedly hampered Russia's refineries, leading to cuts in Russian exports.

Wall Street's boutique investment banks, such as Evercore, Lazard, and Moelis, are facing challenges after hiring many dealmakers in anticipation of a market upswing that has not fully materialized, leading to increased costs.

A resurgence in giant chipmakers and a decline in oil prices contributed to a rebound on Wall Street, with US stocks rising as investors largely disregarded recent hostilities between the US and Iran.
